title: Jobs
scope: Long-running, retryable, cancellable background work: owns the job queue, the dispatcher, and the runner loop, with per-job-type handlers registered by the apps that own them. Schedules can fire a job at its next occurrence via the schedules→jobs trigger. A required core app. NOT the scheduling source itself (Schedules) — Jobs runs the work.

specs.title: Create a job
specs.behavior: Creating a job records a named unit of background work — on-demand or scheduled — with its command, optional schedule, creator, and notify-on-done setting.

specs.title: List jobs
specs.behavior: Listing returns jobs, optionally filtered by status or creator.

specs.title: Update a job
specs.behavior: Updating changes a job's definition — status, name, command, schedule, or notify setting.

specs.title: Run a job
specs.behavior: Running a job executes it manually now via its registered handler and records the result.
